title: Pneumonia follow-up
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports clinician-recorded temperature, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation, mental status, eating or hydration context, and focal chest examination findings without asserting severity or diagnosis.
examination.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-recorded temperature, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation, and chest-auscultation fields without asserting severity.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-recorded temperature, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation, mental status, eating or hydration context, and focal chest examination findings without asserting severity or diagnosis.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2 and 1.6.7 plus terms — clinical observations and signs

investigations.text: Supports no routine post-inpatient follow-up radiograph, consideration at 6 weeks for smoking, age over 50, persistent or deteriorating symptoms, or unexplained weight loss, and shared decision-making using recent imaging, comorbidity, frailty, prognosis, treatment options, and preferences.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-reviewed or clinician-decided follow-up chest-radiograph fields in the qualified post-inpatient context.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports no routine post-inpatient follow-up radiograph, consideration at 6 weeks for smoking, age over 50, persistent or deteriorating symptoms, or unexplained weight loss, and shared decision-making using recent imaging, comorbidity, frailty, prognosis, treatment options, and preferences.

referral.text: Supports reassessment when symptoms or signs do not improve or worsen, clinician-led referral, microbiology where specified, and CRP or procalcitonin review in the qualified hospital treatment-failure context.
referral.rationale: Supports mapped reassessment, persistent signs, referral, and hospital CRP-review fields.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports reassessment when symptoms or signs do not improve or worsen, clinician-led referral, microbiology where specified, and CRP or procalcitonin review in the qualified hospital treatment-failure context.

follow_up.text: Supports review of fever, cough, sputum, breathlessness, chest pain, fatigue or functional recovery, worsening or non-improving symptoms, systemic illness, and clinician-documented safety advice, with adult and child qualifiers preserved.
follow_up.rationale: Supports mapped pneumonia follow-up terms, symptom recovery, assessed negatives, worsening or non-improving prompts, and safety documentation with source qualifiers preserved.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports review of fever, cough, sputum, breathlessness, chest pain, fatigue or functional recovery, worsening or non-improving symptoms, systemic illness, and clinician-documented safety advice, with adult and child qualifiers preserved.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.10.1–1.10.4 — recovery and when to seek further advice

safety_netting.text: Supports clinician recognition of unexplained haemoptysis and age- and smoking-context combinations involving unexplained cough, fatigue, breathlessness, chest pain, weight loss, appetite loss, recurrent chest infection, clubbing, lymphadenopathy, or chest signs.
safety_netting.rationale: Supports the mapped haemoptysis assessed-negative prompt as a respiratory warning feature.
safety_netting.evidence_extract: Supports clinician recognition of unexplained haemoptysis and age- and smoking-context combinations involving unexplained cough, fatigue, breathlessness, chest pain, weight loss, appetite loss, recurrent chest infection, clubbing, lymphadenopathy, or chest signs.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.6 — lung and pleural cancer respiratory features

source_limitations: The legacy workflow does not preserve every age, community-treatment, hospital-treatment, or discharge qualifier.

Chest infection alias, medication adherence, appetite or hydration without child qualifier, chest percussion, generic blood tests, incomplete imaging resolution as a red flag, physiotherapy, generic clinician plan, fixed follow-up ranges, PRN wording, and some follow-up fields remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, severity, imaging interpretation, treatment, referral urgency, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
